key_departures.impact: Signed a 3yr/$63M deal ($42M guaranteed) with the Titans. Posted 7.5 sacks in 2025, 14.5 sacks over two Broncos seasons. Was Denver's most productive interior pass rusher. Denver never made a serious extension offer. Significant hole at IDL/5-tech DE.

Released by Denver with a post-June 1 designation, saving $8.19M in cap space. Played just 8 regular-season games in 2025 due to quad, hamstring injuries, and a suspension. Returned to SF on a 1yr/$7.5M deal. His release creates no meaningful hole given he barely played, and the Broncos immediately re-signed Singleton and Strnad to fill ILB.

Signed a 1yr/$5M deal with Dallas. Was a key backup/spot starter for 7 seasons in Denver; started final 6 regular-season games and both playoff games after Brandon Jones went down. Departure creates a depth hole at S behind Hufanga and Jones.
Fullback departed; replaced by re-signing of Adam Prentice who seized the role mid-2025 and played 45% of special teams snaps.
Veteran depth TE departed; minimal impact given age and role.
Depth center/IOL departed. Minimal impact given Denver's established IOL starters.

contract.notes: Max value $20M with $2M/yr incentive if Dobbins exceeds 1,200 scrimmage yards. 2027 year has no guaranteed money, giving DEN a clean out. Per NBC Sports structure: $4M signing bonus + $4M fully guaranteed 2026 base + $7M non-guaranteed 2027 base.

signings.analysis: Dobbins led the Broncos in rushing (772 yards, 5.0 YPC in 10 games) before a Lisfranc injury ended his 2025. The 2yr/$16M base deal ($20M max) is structured as a bridge starter — only 2026 is fully guaranteed. Meaningful investment for the position but it doesn't fully resolve RB depth given Dobbins' chronic injury history (has not played a full season since 2020). Denver still has RJ Harvey, Tyler Badie, and McLaughlin, but RB remains a modest draft priority.

contract.notes: $3M signing bonus, $3.99M fully guaranteed 2026 base, $3.5M guaranteed in 2027. $11M total fully guaranteed.

signings.analysis: Singleton led the Broncos with 135 tackles in 2025 and is the defensive green-dot/signal caller. The 2yr/$15.5M deal is mid-market for a veteran starting ILB with $11M guaranteed — real investment. However, at 32, he's not a long-term solution. Denver will still look to draft a linebacker. The release of Greenlaw means Singleton and Strnad are the clear starting pair, keeping LB a moderate draft need for developmental depth.

Strnad's 3yr/$18M deal ($10M guaranteed) signals a genuine multi-year commitment to him as a starter. He has earned starter-level snaps alongside Singleton and fits Vance Joseph's defense. Combined with Singleton's re-signing, LB is now a depth/development need rather than a glaring hole. The 3-year structure is the key indicator here — team sees Strnad as a foundational piece.

contract.notes: Max value $18.5M with $1.5M in performance incentives (catches, yards).

signings.analysis: Trautman's 3yr/$17M deal ($9.5M guaranteed) is below the starter tier for TEs (AAV ~$5.7M). He is Payton's trusted blocking TE and led the position group in snap share (57%), but had only 20 catches in 2025. He is NOT a dynamic receiving threat. Denver still has Evan Engram (underperformed in 2025, potential release candidate) and Nate Adkins. The TE room remains a concern for offensive production — Denver did not upgrade meaningfully here. TE remains a real draft priority.
Minimum-level re-sign. Versatile blocker/FB-TE hybrid provides positional flexibility but does not address the need for a receiving TE. No impact on TE need tier.
Veteran minimum re-sign for roster depth. No impact on need tier.

contract.notes: Max $11M with incentives. $4.075M fully guaranteed in 2026, $1.5M guaranteed in 2027.

signings.analysis: Palczewski started 10 games over two seasons and plays 58% of offensive snaps as a versatile backup. The 2yr/$9.5M deal is well above minimum, signaling genuine roster value. However, he is still a backup behind an established starting IOL group (Meinerz, Powers, Wattenberg). No change to IOL need tier — Denver's starters are locked in.
Minimum re-sign. Adds rotational depth (5.1 YPC in 2025) but does not change the need picture at RB. Denver declined to tender him as RFA, then re-signed at minimum. Depth only.

Near-minimum re-sign. Payton-favorite blocker/spot receiver (9 catches, 1 TD in 7 games in 2025). Does not address WR need in any meaningful way — Denver still lacks a true WR2 opposite Courtland Sutton.
Fullback re-sign replaces departed Michael Burton at near-minimum. Played 19% offensive snaps and 45% special teams snaps in 2025. Maintains roster continuity. No need impact.
Backup QB re-sign behind Bo Nix. Low-cost, no need impact. Stidham trade calls have been reported but nothing materialized.

Minimum re-sign for DL depth following Franklin-Myers departure. Henningsen missed all of 2025 with a torn Achilles suffered in preseason. Provides developmental depth but does NOT address the starter vacancy left by Franklin-Myers. IDL remains a significant draft need.

contract.notes: Second-round RFA tender. McMillian signed the tender; Denver retains full rights. He becomes an unrestricted free agent in 2027.

signings.analysis: The 2nd-round tender keeps McMillian for 2026 at $5.81M and retains Denver's rights to match any offer sheet. He played a key secondary role alongside Riley Moss in 2025. However, it's a one-year, team-controlled arrangement — not a long-term commitment. Denver will face a decision on McMillian vs. Moss vs. 2025 1st-round pick Jahdae Barron going forward. CB need partially addressed for 2026 but remains a future consideration.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: John Franklin-Myers (7.5 sacks, 14.5 over two DEN seasons) signed a 3yr/$63M deal with the Titans. Denver's only IDL addition was a minimum re-sign of Matt Henningsen, who missed all of 2025 with an Achilles tear. Uwazurike and DJ Jones are the depth options; there is no starter-caliber replacement in house. IDL escalates to a critical top-5 draft need — Denver must address this in rounds 1-3.

PJ Locke departed to Dallas on a 1yr/$5M deal. Hufanga (2nd-team All-Pro) and Brandon Jones (coming back from shoulder injury) are the starters, but depth is thin (Devon Key, JL Skinner). Multiple sources indicate Denver plans to address S in the draft. Need escalates from depth to genuine priority — rounds 2-4 likely.

Re-signing both Alex Singleton (2yr/$15.5M, $11M gtd) and Justin Strnad (3yr/$18M, $10M gtd) solidifies the starting ILB pair. Greenlaw's release was expected given injuries. Strnad's 3-year deal is a genuine commitment. LB drops from a top need to a developmental/depth need — team will still likely draft a LB in rounds 2-4 to develop behind aging starters.

Trautman's 3yr/$17M re-sign is below the starter TE market (AAV $5.7M). He is a blocking TE who caught 20 passes in 2025. Evan Engram underperformed in 2025 and his future is uncertain. Denver did NOT sign an outside TE despite interest in Goedert (extended by PHI) and others who went elsewhere. TE remains a meaningful draft need — a receiving-capable TE is still very much on the team's wish list in rounds 1-4.

Zero WR additions in free agency. Denver had interest in Romeo Doubs (went to New England on $70M deal), Kendrick Bourne (signed with Arizona), and Jailen Nailor (went to Las Vegas). Courtland Sutton is locked in, but Marvin Mims Jr. is the only other real option. WR remains a critical offensive need heading into the draft — unchanged.

Dobbins re-sign (2yr/$16M base, $8M guaranteed) retains the starter. Denver also returns RJ Harvey, Tyler Badie, and McLaughlin — the same top-4 from 2025. However, Dobbins has not played a full season since 2020 and his 2027 year is fully unguaranteed. RB drops slightly in urgency but a developmental/complement back via mid-rounds draft is still sensible.

McMillian's 2nd-round RFA tender ($5.81M) retains him for 2026. Riley Moss and Jahdae Barron (2025 1st-round pick) are also on the roster. CB depth is adequate for 2026 but a future decision looms — Denver likely cannot keep all three at starter money. Need remains at tier 3 — a depth/developmental addition in the mid-rounds would make sense.

key_additions.headline: Starting RB retained after leading Denver in rushing (772 yards, 5.0 YPC) before Lisfranc injury ended 2025 season
Team captain and leading tackler (135) re-signed as the defensive green-dot signal caller for Vance Joseph's system
Breakout starter locked up on 3-year deal to pair with Singleton at ILB through at least 2028
Payton's trusted blocking TE re-signed on 3-year deal; played 57% of offensive snaps in 2025
Versatile swing lineman secured through 2027 after starting 10 games across two seasons
Secondary playmaker retained on 2nd-round tender; will compete for starting CB role alongside Moss and Barron in 2026

key_losses.headline: Interior pass rush anchor (7.5 sacks in 2025, 14.5 over 2 DEN seasons) departs on elite IDL deal; biggest hole of the offseason
7-year Broncos veteran and clutch playoff starter departs; creates depth void at safety behind Hufanga and Jones
Marquee 2025 signing released after injury-shortened season (8 games); Broncos save $8.19M in cap space post-June 1
